brief.speaker: carol
brief.text: Same query. Drop info-level. Negation in DDSQL is a leading hyphen on the tag — -level:info. Same way you'd remove a term from a search.

expected.sql: SELECT * FROM logs WHERE tags['service'] = 'auth-svc' AND tags['env'] = 'prod' AND tags['level'] != 'info'

hints.text: Carol: 'Still noise in there. The hyphen before the tag excludes it — -level:info.'
Carol: 'Too few. service:auth-svc env:prod -level:info — three conditions.'
Carol: 'Query error. Three tags: service:auth-svc env:prod -level:info. Leading hyphen is the negation.'
Carol: 'service:auth-svc env:prod -level:info — the hyphen means NOT. Three tags, third one excluded.'

success.speaker: carol
success.text: Thirty rows. Now we can actually read them.
